params.name: amount
params.description: The transaction amount in the **smallest currency unit**. For Ghana, pass pesewas (1 GHS = 100 pesewas); for Nigeria, pass kobo (1 NGN = 100 kobo); for Zambia, pass ngwe (1 ZMW = 100 ngwe). Must be a positive integer.

params.name: country
params.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the transaction. Determines which banking network Vesicash routes through. Example: `GH` for Ghana, `NG` for Nigeria, `ZM` for Zambia, `KE` for Kenya.

params.name: currency
params.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. Must match the country you specified. Example: `GHS` for Ghana Cedis, `NGN` for Nigerian Naira, `ZMW` for Zambian Kwacha.

params.name: method
params.description: The payment channel to use. Set to `"card"` to process a card payment through the Mastercard Payment Gateway.

params.name: operator
params.description: The payment processor to route through. Use `"mpgs"` for Mastercard Payment Gateway Services, which supports 3DS2 card processing across all supported markets.

params.name: narration
params.description: A short description of what the customer is paying for. Displayed on the Vesicash checkout page and included on the customer's bank statement. Maximum 100 characters.

params.name: redirect_success_url
params.description: A publicly accessible URL on your server. Vesicash redirects the customer here after a successful payment, appending `?payment_reference=<ref>` as a query parameter. Must be HTTPS in production.

params.name: redirect_failed_url
params.description: A publicly accessible URL on your server. Vesicash redirects the customer here if the payment fails or is abandoned. Use this to show an appropriate retry or support message.

params.name: webhook_url
params.description: A publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint on your server. Vesicash POSTs a signed JSON payload to this URL whenever the transaction status changes. Always verify the signature using your webhook secret.

params.name: integration_type
params.description: Defines the integration mode. Set to `"DIRECT_API"` for all server-to-server integrations. This controls whether Vesicash uses a hosted checkout page or returns raw payment data for your own UI.

blocks.content:
Same endpoint as the checkout flow, but returns a `payment_reference` (not a `payment_link`) which you use in the next step to submit card details directly.

---
**Note:** In the non-checkout flow, `data.reference` is the `payment_reference` you pass to the next step. There is no `payment_link`.
